summary: The synthetic temporal anomaly packet is a promising reusable lane for before/after review, but it needs stronger caveats around occupancy inference and automated action readiness.

findings.title: Occupancy inference exceeds temporal evidence
findings.summary: The packet moves from one generic shape change to a claim that a person entered the space, but the synthetic evidence only supports anomaly follow-up.

findings.title: Automated action readiness is unsupported
findings.summary: The packet proposes automated household action without a caller-owned gate, approval note, or stronger evidence boundary.

findings.title: Temporal evidence labels stay reusable
findings.summary: The frame labels and zone references remain generic enough for public-safe fixture maintenance and repeat comparison coverage.

decision.summary: The synthetic temporal anomaly packet is a promising reusable lane for before/after review, but it needs stronger caveats around occupancy inference and automated action readiness.

non_blocking_findings.title: Occupancy inference exceeds temporal evidence
non_blocking_findings.summary: The packet moves from one generic shape change to a claim that a person entered the space, but the synthetic evidence only supports anomaly follow-up.

non_blocking_findings.title: Automated action readiness is unsupported
non_blocking_findings.summary: The packet proposes automated household action without a caller-owned gate, approval note, or stronger evidence boundary.

non_blocking_findings.title: Temporal evidence labels stay reusable
non_blocking_findings.summary: The frame labels and zone references remain generic enough for public-safe fixture maintenance and repeat comparison coverage.

follow_up: Keep the shared artifact focused on generic temporal anomaly follow-up instead of occupancy or identity conclusions.
Retain explicit caller ownership for any downstream alerting, storage, or household action.
Continue only if future temporal fixtures can stay apartment-agnostic and comparison-focused.
